reasoning: Enterobiasis (pinworm infection) is a common, mild parasitic infection that is readily treated with inexpensive drugs like mebendazole or albendazole. Because of the ease of treatment and the low morbidity of the disease, there is virtually no economic or public health incentive to develop a vaccine. A search of recent literature and clinical trials reveals that vaccine research for pinworm is, at best, in highly 'nascent stages' with no candidates in clinical trials. Since vaccine development and approval safely takes at least 5-10 years, and typically decades for poorly-funded diseases, it is near-impossible for a vaccine to be developed, approved, and listed on the Wikipedia page by any of the target dates within the next six months (Aug 2025 to Jan 2026). Probabilities are set to the minimum allowed bound (0.05) for near-term dates and increase slightly for the later dates solely to reflect structural uncertainty (e.g., Wikipedia vandalism incorrectly triggering resolution).
